author: haiku-multi-issue-gen <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Landed multi-issue plan generator + builder in
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/proptest_harness.rs:212-376` and a
new Property 1b at `proptest_harness.rs:457-509`. Verified:

- `cargo test --release -p jjf-storage --test proptest_harness`:
  5 passed in 11.73s (well under the 30s budget).
- `PROPTEST_CASES=128 cargo test --release ... --test proptest_harness`:
  5 passed in 101.59s — generator + builder hold up under 8x cases.
- `cargo nextest run --workspace`: 616 passed, no regressions.
  `round_trip_multi_issue_with_deps` runs in ~21s with the default 16 cases.

Design choices worth flagging:

- **Plan/build split.** The strategy emits a pure
  `MultiIssuePlan` (drafts + index-pair-keyed edges). The repo
  materializes inside the test body via `build_multi_issue_repo`.
  This keeps the shrinker side-effect-free and avoids re-running
  shell-outs per shrink candidate.
- **Cycle-free by construction.** Every edge has
  `child_idx > parent_idx` (strict upper triangle), so the
  resulting graph is a DAG and `add_dep_edge`'s cycle preflight
  never fires. The cycle-rejection path is left to ticket `1078439`.
- **Slugs forced to `None`** in the multi-issue draft. With the
  4-element `SLUG_POOL` and N up to 4, `SlugCollision` would
  otherwise dominate the create-loop outcome and force a `Result`-
  swallow path. Slug coverage stays on Property 1.

## Recommendation

Both blocked tickets (`6ce795c proptest-list-ready-monotone`,
`1078439 proptest-cycle-rejection`) can now build on
`multi_issue_plan_strategy` and `build_multi_issue_repo`
directly. For the cycle property: generate a base plan, then
emit ONE extra `(parent_idx, child_idx, kind)` triple — the
reverse-direction edge that closes a cycle — and assert
`add_dep_edge` returns `Error::DependencyCycle`. For the
ready-monotone property: build a plan, snapshot
`list_ready`, close one issue (or `add_dep_edge` a new
blocking edge), snapshot again, and assert the new set
only differs by predictable additions/removals.

## Confidence

high. The new property passes under both default and 8x
stress; full workspace stays green; the design follows the
existing harness conventions (`fresh_scratch_repo`, `pin_clock`,
bounded input pools).

## Open follow-ups

- none. Both downstream tickets are filed and now unblocked.
